data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260507T030003-f2024896/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260507T030003-f2024896/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

If you write `.ddx/executions/20260507T030003-f2024896/no_changes_rationale.txt`, it MUST carry one of:

- `verification_command: <one-line shell command>` — the loop runs it (cwd = project root, 60s); exit 0 closes the bead, non-zero rejects.
- `status: needs_investigation` + `reason: <why>` — bead stays open for triage.

Bare rationales ("nothing to do") are rejected.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260507T030003-f2024896/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260507T030003-f2024896/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Bead overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and conservative project defaults (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) in this worktree — write whatever the bead asks for.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-cf37253d">
    <title>spec: define full DDx temp cleanup in work cycle</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
The cleanup/resource epic now has a cleanup manager, operator command, resource preflight, loop-fatal resource_exhausted handling, and periodic work cleanup, but the spec still lets the implementation interpret DDx-owned cleanup too narrowly. The current work cycle cleaned the execution worktree root while DDx-created test/e2e scratch roots accumulated until the host temp filesystem hit 100%.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:202-212 scans only the configured execution temp root and only directories whose names start with ExecuteBeadWtPrefix. DDx-owned e2e/test scratch prefixes are outside that contract.
- cli/internal/agent/execution_resources.go:13-16 sets the hard free-space floor to 64 MiB and 1024 inodes, which is too low to prevent degraded work-cycle behavior on a shared temp filesystem.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:501-503 and 550-551 run cleanup at startup and pre-claim, but the spec does not require those passes to reclaim all DDx-owned scratch roots or to apply a soft high-water policy before claims.
- docs/helix/06-iterate/reliability-principles.md:47-54 states DDx-owned resources must be reclaimed, but FEAT-010/SD-025 need explicit temp-prefix scope, thresholds, and work-cycle semantics.

PROPOSED FIX
- Update FEAT-010 and SD-025 so DDx-owned cleanup includes execution worktrees, DDx-created e2e/test scratch roots, generated test binaries, and run-state/liveness files.
- Define a conservative scratch-root policy: only DDx-owned prefixes or metadata-marked paths, minimum age/mtime threshold, no live process/liveness, preserve evidence and registered active worktrees.
- Define work-cycle behavior: ddx work and server-managed workers run one cleanup pass before first claim and before subsequent claims when temp free space is below a soft high-water threshold; if still below the hard floor after cleanup, stop with resource_exhausted.
- Define operator reporting fields for scratch roots removed, bytes/inodes reclaimed, preserved paths, and blocked warnings.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement code changes in this spec bead.
- Do not permit broad deletion of arbitrary non-DDx temp directories.
- Do not change provider quota, routing, or review policy.

DEPS
Depends on the closed cleanup foundation beads under ddx-f3dfde0d; no new code dependency.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md defines DDx-owned scratch cleanup scope beyond execution worktrees and names the conservative ownership/liveness rules.
2. docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-025-task-execution-lifecycle.md defines soft high-water and hard floor resource behavior for ddx work/server workers before claim.
3. docs/helix/06-iterate/reliability-principles.md P8 is updated or cross-referenced so leaked DDx test/e2e scratch roots are explicitly in scope.
4. The spec names expected implementation tests: TestExecutionCleanup_RemovesStaleDDXScratchDirs, TestWorkResourcePreflight_RunsCleanupBelowSoftFloor, and TestWorkResourcePreflight_StopsBelowHardFloorAfterCleanup.
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:2, area:docs, area:agent, kind:spec, reliability, cleanup, resource-exhaustion, spec:FEAT-010, spec:SD-025</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-f3dfde0d" spec-id="FEAT-010" base-rev="99312ff6a845622e05e30be81e31cb836c3a53c6" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260507T030003-f2024896"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <ref id="FEAT-010" path="docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md">Feature: Task Execution</ref>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
Implemented the spec update and committed it as `92ed74563` (`docs: define DDx temp cleanup scope and thresholds [ddx-cf37253d]`).

What changed:
- [P8 in `docs/helix/06-iterate/reliability-principles.md`](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-cf37253d-20260507T030003-f2024896/docs/helix/06-iterate/reliability-principles.md#L47-L52) now explicitly includes leaked DDx test/e2e scratch roots, generated test binaries, and run-state/liveness files in DDx-owned cleanup scope.
- [FEAT-010 execution cleanup](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-cf37253d-20260507T030003-f2024896/docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md#L488-L506) now defines the conservative ownership/liveness rules and DDx-owned scratch-root scope.
- [FEAT-010 requirements](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-cf37253d-20260507T030003-f2024896/docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md#L722-L735) now specify the soft high-water and hard floor work-cycle behavior and name the expected implementation tests.
- [SD-025 cleanup design](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-cf37253d-20260507T030003-f2024896/docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-025-task-execution-lifecycle.md#L224-L307) now mirrors the scratch-root scope, conservative deletion rules, and pre-claim cleanup behavior.

Verification:
- `lefthook run pre-commit` passed.

The worktree is clean.
